description_long: This object is a historical-style cannon mounted on a wooden carriage with four solid wheels. The cannon barrel is made of metal, showing a weathered, rusty surface, and features a flared muzzle and reinforcing rings along its length. The carriage is constructed from thick wooden planks, cut into a blocky, robust shape, and supports the heavy barrel with two side trunnions. The wheels are simple, solid discs, also made of wood, and are attached to axles that allow the cannon to be repositioned. The rear of the carriage has a protruding handle or tiller for maneuvering. The overall appearance suggests a piece of artillery from the pre-modern era, designed for use in sieges or defensive positions.
description: A weathered metal cannon mounted on a sturdy wooden carriage with four solid wheels, evoking historical artillery.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular muzzle of the cannon and the symmetrical arrangement of the wooden carriage and wheels.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the cannon barrel, the trunnions, and the rectangular wooden carriage with two visible wheels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back end of the cannon barrel, the carriage's rear structure, and all four wheels aligned.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the cannon barrel, carriage, and wheels from the other profile, with the tiller handle visible at the rear.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed cannon barrel mounted centrally on the wooden carriage.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; surfaces are solid brown and rust colors.
